title: Create Snapgram Social Media App

requirements.text: Replace the existing shinchan diary backend with a new Motoko single-actor backend for a social media app named 'snapgram'. The backend must support: user profiles (displayName, bio, avatarUrl, createdAt), posts (id, authorPrincipal, content up to 500 chars, imageUrl optional, createdAt, likesCount), follow relationships (follower/following principals), likes (postId + principal), and comments (id, postId, authorPrincipal, content, createdAt). Expose query and update methods for: createProfile, getProfile, updateProfile, createPost, getPosts (paginated feed), getPostsByUser, likePost, unlikePost, followUser, unfollowUser, getFollowers, getFollowing, addComment, getComments. All write operations must be gated to authenticated (non-anonymous) callers.

quotes: I want to make social media app name is snapgram

acceptanceCriteria: User profiles can be created and retrieved by principal
Posts can be created, listed (feed), and listed by user
Like and unlike operations are idempotent and correctly tracked
Follow and unfollow operations are recorded and queryable
Comments can be added to posts and retrieved
Anonymous callers are rejected on all write methods
Backend compiles and deploys without errors

requirements.text: Build a complete React + TypeScript frontend for Snapgram with the following pages and features: (1) Landing/Login page with the Snapgram brand name and Internet Identity sign-in, (2) Home feed showing posts from followed users and own posts with infinite or paginated scroll, (3) Explore/Discover page showing all recent posts, (4) Create Post page (text content up to 500 characters, optional image URL), (5) User Profile page showing avatar, display name, bio, follower/following counts, and the user's own posts with follow/unfollow button for other users, (6) Post detail view with comments and like button, (7) Edit Profile page (displayName, bio, avatarUrl). Gate all pages except Landing behind authentication using the existing RequireAuth component.

quotes: I want to make social media app name is snapgram

acceptanceCriteria: Landing page displays 'Snapgram' brand name and Internet Identity login button
Authenticated users see a home feed page
Users can create a post with text content
Users can view another user's profile and follow or unfollow them
Post detail page shows all comments and a like button
Edit profile form saves changes successfully
Unauthenticated users are redirected to the landing/login page for all protected routes
Loading states and error messages are shown during data operations

requirements.text: Wire all frontend Snapgram pages to the backend using React Query. Implement hooks for: useProfile, useFeed, useExplorePosts, useUserPosts, useCreatePost, useLikePost, useFollowUser, useAddComment, useComments. Include loading spinners, error toasts, and optimistic or invalidation-based cache updates after mutations.

quotes: I want to make social media app name is snapgram

acceptanceCriteria: Feed updates automatically after creating a post
Like count updates immediately after liking or unliking
Follow/unfollow status reflects correctly after action
Comments list refreshes after adding a comment
Error messages appear on failed operations
Loading indicators are shown while data is fetching

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual theme for Snapgram inspired by modern social media aesthetics. Use a warm gradient-accented color palette (e.g., warm pinks, oranges, or sunset tones) — not blue or purple. Design a clean card-based layout for posts, a sticky top navigation bar with the Snapgram logo, bottom tab navigation on mobile viewports, and smooth hover/focus transitions. Use Tailwind CSS and shadcn components consistently across all pages.

quotes: I want to make social media app name is snapgram

acceptanceCriteria: All pages share a consistent color palette and typography
Navigation bar is visible and functional on all authenticated pages
Post cards have a uniform visual style across feed, explore, and profile pages
No blue or purple primary colors are used
The app is usable on both desktop and mobile viewports

requirements.text: Generate and display brand images for Snapgram: a logo mark used in the navigation header, and a hero illustration for the landing page. Store images as static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly in the frontend components.

quotes: I want to make social media app name is snapgram

acceptanceCriteria: Snapgram logo appears in the navigation header on all authenticated pages
Hero illustration appears on the landing/login page
Images load correctly in production builds
No backend routes are used to serve the images

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in a single actor file (backend/main.mo)
Do not edit files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Use existing Internet Identity auth infrastructure (useInternetIdentity hook and RequireAuth component)
Do not use blue or purple as the primary color palette
nonGoals: Third-party OAuth or social login (only Internet Identity)
Preserving or migrating the existing shinchan diary data model

required: Snapgram wordmark logo with a warm gradient camera or spark icon, suitable for a navigation bar (snapgram-logo.dim_320x80.png)
Warm sunset gradient hero illustration with abstract social connection motif for a landing page (snapgram-hero.dim_1200x600.png)
